zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Doggett於1481年1月1日出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可格洛托時，他的父親Andrew Doggett27歲，母親Katherine Duckett20歲。他於1509年與Sarah Ann LeGris在英國英格蘭素福爾可格洛托結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1526年在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆享年45歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可格洛托。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Doggett于1481年1月1日出生在英国英格兰素福爾可格洛托时，他的父亲Andrew Doggett27岁，母亲Katherine Duckett20岁。他于1509年与Sarah Ann LeGris在英国英格兰素福爾可格洛托结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1526年在英国素福爾可拉文哈姆享年45岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可格洛托。

nameMeanings.name: Doggett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Duckett with voicing of the medial consonant. Alternatively, the name may also be from a Middle English rhyming pet form of Roger (see Dodge ) + the diminutive suffix -et. But whether the medial -g- was pronounced as a stop /g/ (as in dog) or an affricate /dʒ/ (as in Roger) is impossible to tell.

variants.name: Duckett
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Duket. This may sometimes have been an early Middle English pet form of an Old English Ducc(a), but was more often a pet form of Marmaduke, especially in Yorkshire (see Duke ). Marmaduke itself is from Irish Maol Maedóc ‘servant of Maedóc’, Maedóc being the name of prominent Irish saints of the 6th and 7th centuries.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Doggett was born on 1 January 1481, in Groton, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Andrew Doggett, was 27 and his mother, Katherine Duckett, was 20. He married Sarah Ann LeGris in 1509, in Groton, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 7 daughters. He died in 1526, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, at the age of 45, and was buried in Groton, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Doggett est né le 1 janvier 1481, à Groton, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Andrew Doggett, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Katherine Duckett, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Ann LeGris en 1509, à Groton, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé en 1526, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterré à Groton, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.Bures Saint Mary, Suffolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버스 세인트 메리
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Doggett(이)가 1481년 1월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 그로턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Andrew Doggett(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Duckett(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1509년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 그로턴에서 Sarah Ann LeGris(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1526년 영국 수폴크 레이븐함에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 그로턴에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Sarah Ann LeGris em 1509, em Groton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 7 filhas. ru.Bures Saint Mary, Suffolk, England: буресь сеинт мэри, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Doggett родился 1 января 1481 г. в местности гротон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Andrew Doggett было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Duckett, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Ann LeGris в 1509 г. в местности гротон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 7 дочерей. Он умер в 1526 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 45 лет и был похоронен в местности гротон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.
